reason: Active PRD for OpenList/Alist local scraping, standard/non-standard detection, controlled classification settings, local cache, and MVP scope.
Player architecture source for DataSource abstraction, raw file source scraping flow, local metadata cache, poster wall, and classification settings design.
Security constraints for credentials, token redaction, local cache safety, provider URLs, and no secret leakage to metadata/AI/logs.
Repository-level project rules including raw file source scraping boundaries and no fixed physical movie/tv folder assumptions.
Frontend pre-development checklist and required Player-independent implementation rules.
Code-spec contract for raw file source scraping/classification, DataSource boundaries, source root containment, and no remote writes.
UI contract for settings, controlled scraping classification editor, media cards, poster wall behavior, and Cinema OS component style.
Pinia/settings/credential/cache state boundaries, source-scoped cache behavior, and sensitive config persistence rules.
DataSource and media type contracts, runtime validation expectations, and safe provider response mapping.
Composable/service boundaries for data fetching, Tauri IPC, external API calls, and user-safe errors.
Required validation commands, Windows packaging expectations, and forbidden patterns for raw source scraping.
Scraping work crosses provider API, parsing, local storage, settings, and UI display layers.
Implementation should reuse existing DataSource, settings, media card/grid, and persistence patterns rather than duplicating them.
